sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὀψέ

- Transliteration: Opse

- Phonetic: op-seh'

- Definition:

1. after a long time, long after, late

a. late in the day, i.e. at evening

b. the sabbath having just passed, after the sabbath

1. at the early dawn of the first day of the week

- Origin: from the same as G3694 (through the idea of backwardness)

- TDNT entry: (adverbially) late in the day

- Part(s) of speech:

- Strong's: From the same as G3694 (through the idea of backwardness); (adverbially) late in the day; by extension after the close of the day: - (at) even in the end.
